title: Lightly Baked Indian Reblochon Bake
description: A tangy and springy one-pot Indian dish featuring reblochon, perfect for pescatarian diets, baked to perfection in a wood-fired oven.
instructions: Preheat the wood-fired oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, mix together the basmati rice, chopped onion, grated ginger, garam masala, turmeric, and salt.
In a separate pan, heat some oil and saute the shrimp until they are pink and cooked through.
Add the cooked shrimp to the bowl with the rice mixture and stir well.
In a greased baking dish, create a layer of the rice and shrimp mixture.
Arrange a layer of sliced reblochon cheese on top of the rice mixture.
Repeat the layers until all the ingredients are used up, ending with a layer of cheese on top.
Drizzle the lemon juice over the cheese and sprinkle with chopped cilantro.
Bake the dish in the preheated oven for 20 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Remove the dish from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
